{"step_by_step_thinking": "The tibialis anterior muscle is responsible for dorsiflexion and inversion of the foot. The tibialis posterior muscle is also involved in foot inversion. The peroneus longus muscle, on the other hand, is responsible for eversion and plantarflexion of the foot. The extensor hallucis longus muscle is responsible for extending the big toe and does not contribute to foot inversion. Therefore, the muscle that does not work for inversion of the foot is the extensor hallucis longus.", "answer_choice": "A"}
